name: Hazelnut Caramels
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 9-inch square baking pan with 2-inch high sides with heavy duty aluminum foil, extending foil over the sides of the pan. Butter the foil.
2. Melt 1 cup butter in a heavy large saucepan over low heat. Add both sugars, corn syrup, and sweetened condensed milk and stir until sugar dissolves.
3. Scrape seeds from vanilla bean into mixture; add bean. Attach a clip on candy thermometer to the side of the saucepan.
4. Increase heat to medium and bring mixture to a boil. Cook mixture for 8 minutes, stirring frequently.
5. Using tongs, remove vanilla bean and discard. Continue cooking until candy thermometer registers 242°F, stirring frequently, about 5 minutes longer.
6. Immediately remove from heat; stir in hazelnuts. Quickly pour caramel into prepared pan (do not scrape saucepan). Cool until almost firm, about 1 hour.
7. Using a buttered heavy large knife, score 8 lines lengthwise to a depth of 1/4 inch in caramel, then score 8 lines crosswise. Using the same buttered heavy large knife, cut caramel on scored lines into pieces.
8. Wrap each piece of caramel in colored cellophane/plastic wrap.
9. Can be made 2 weeks ahead. Store in an airtight container.
